growth_records.medium: GAM (Gifu Anaerobic Medium, Nissui Pharmaceuticals) + GalNAc

up.method: 13C-[UL-13C6]GalNAc tracer + GC-MS; qualitative demonstration of GalNAc import/catabolism (13C label appears in central-carbon metabolites); no mmol/gDW/h reported

sec.units: not quantified as a rate (only 13C-enrichment reported: ~17% of lactate labelled)

mfa.note: After 13 h culture on 13C-GalNAc; product of tagatose-1,6-bisphosphate cleavage by GatZ aldolase; fractional labelling, NOT a net flux in mmol/gDW/h
After 13 h culture on 13C-GalNAc; other product of tagatose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase (GatZ); fractional labelling
Downstream lower-glycolysis intermediate labelled from GalNAc; fractional labelling
Terminal fermentation product labelled from GalNAc; fractional labelling

growth_records.cite: Yang Hui et al. ABO genotype alters the gut microbiota by regulating GalNAc levels in pigs. Nature (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41586-022-04769-z. [PMC9157047]
growth_records.snippet: After 13 h of culture, around 29% of glyceraldehyde-3-P and around 25% of dihydoxyacetone phosphate (the products of the conversion of tagatose-1,6-bisphosphate by gatZ aldolase) were labelled with 13C ... Approximately 23% of 3-phosphoglyc
growth_records.notes: This is a gut-microbiome/host-genetics Nature paper, NOT a dedicated GEM/13C-MFA study, but its Methods section 'Determining the metabolic flux ratios from 13C experiments by GC-MS' provides genuine EXPERIMENTAL prokaryote flux-tracer data. Strain 4-8-110 (OTU476-like) cultured in 6 ml GAM medium + 1 g/L GalNAc (13C-[UL-13C6] tracer vs 12C control), anaerobic glovebox (10% H2/10% CO2/80% N2), 37C, 13 h, triplicate. GC-MS-measured 13C-enrichment of central-carbon metabolites: GAP ~29%, DHAP ~25%, 3PG ~23%, lactate ~17% (mapping the GalNAc->tagatose-6-P->tagatose-1,6-bisP->GAP/DHAP->lower glycolysis->lactate pathway via GatZ aldolase). These are FRACTIONAL LABELLING proportions, NOT net fluxes and NOT rates in mmol/gDW/h; recorded in mfa_fluxes with units '% of pool labelled'. NO substrate uptake rate, NO product secretion rate (only lactate 13C-enrichment), and NO growth rate in mmol/gDW/h or per h are reported. Growth timing: OTU476-like strains reach end of exponential phase at ~11 h and were cultured 13 h; E. coli reference reached end of exponential phase at ~4 h; OD600 measured by spectrophotometer but no OD-vs-time series or mu given, so no growth_rate_per_h derivable (no arithmetic possible). pH not stated (GAM ~pH 7). Medium is complex/undefined (GAM), so medium_composition_stated=false; the only defined addition is GalNAc at 1 g/L. No numbers invented.
